fills: Tabel 4.8 (ADC classification) + Tabel 4.10 (per-dim mean/std/range/nonzero-frac, N=96,727) + Tabel 4.9 (threshold-sensitivity) + selector geometry
INHERITANCE_DISCLOSURE: MANDATORY FRAMING — these JT magnitudes are 3-learner-runtime-driven. The sealed Kalman anchor re-states canonical_mastery_after = m_K (single Kalman) and uses the ensemble only for sigma^2, but the recorded JT signals / ADC / governance distributions in experiment_trajectories were inherited from the V1 runtime, which used the 3-learner mean (m_K+m_B+m_S)/3 as the mastery that drove governance. So every governance/JT magnitude below reflects 3-learner-driven governance, NOT m_K-driven. The ADC *verdicts* (active/dormant) are robust to this (they key on signal presence/dispersion, not absolute mastery); only the magnitudes (4.9/4.10/4.28/4.29) genuinely differ. An exact m_K-driven regenerate is UNAVAILABLE here (would need a from-ingest re-run: ephemeral normalization state not persisted + per-learner mastery_before not persisted) and is not load-bearing for the core claims. Source: research_validation/KALMAN_OVERLAY_GOVERNANCE_SCOPING.md.

thresholds_sealed.definition: ALPHA_FLOOR = minimum mean contribution; SIGNAL_RATIO = minimum std/mean dispersion. ACTIVE iff (mean > ALPHA_FLOOR) AND (std/mean > SIGNAL_RATIO) AND nonzero signal present.
thresholds_sealed.source: sealed_runs.frozen_stats.thresholds for seal-bae44d1a; SQL: SELECT frozen_stats FROM sealed_runs WHERE seal_id='seal-bae44d1a-a314-48f5-b145-c92e6cbe08d7'

TWO_DISTINCT_SUBSTRATES_WARNING: There are TWO different per-dimension number sets and they must NOT be conflated. (A) The jt_*_contribution COLUMNS in experiment_trajectories (the decomposed contribution magnitudes) — queried directly below as 'tabel_4_10_per_dim_stats_COLUMNS'. (B) The sealer's own activation_profile, computed by adaptive_dimension_controller.classify_dimension over raw_governance_snapshot (the normalized governance WEIGHTS, a different quantity) — reproduced below as 'sealer_activation_profile_SNAPSHOT'. They give different magnitudes (e.g. challenge column mean=0.158 but snapshot mean=0.0; transfer_realized column mean=0.015 but snapshot mean=0.153) because they measure different things. For the thesis Tabel 4.10 the COLUMN stats are the per-dimension contribution distribution; for the Tabel 4.8 ADC verdict the SNAPSHOT classifier is the sealed authoritative classification.

tabel_4_10_per_dim_stats_COLUMNS._source: READ-ONLY SQL over experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id='run-d2154070-b6e4-4abe-94e6-a2e6ccfbefc1' (N=96,727). avg=mean, stddev_pop=population std, nonzero_fraction = frac of rows with value<>0, count_fraction = count(col)/96727.
tabel_4_10_per_dim_stats_COLUMNS._columns: metric | count | mean | std_pop | min | max | nonzero_fraction | non_null_fraction

tabel_4_8_ADC_classification_OVER_COLUMN._note: Classification applying the SEALED thresholds (ALPHA_FLOOR=0.01 mean, SIGNAL_RATIO=0.08 std/mean) DIRECTLY to the jt_*_contribution column stats above (NOT the sealer's snapshot classifier). signal_ratio = std_pop/mean. ACTIVE iff mean>0.01 AND signal_ratio>0.08 AND nonzero present. Provided for the contribution-column view; for the authoritative sealed verdict see sealer_activation_profile_SNAPSHOT.

jt_transfer_prospective_contribution.rationale: structural zero: hardcoded 0.0 at the call site (unified_brain.py:2512), no substrate signal

tabel_4_8_ADC_classification_OVER_COLUMN.summary: 5 of 6 ACTIVE; only transfer_prospective DORMANT (structural zero). NOTE this column-based verdict differs from the sealer snapshot verdict for challenge (here ACTIVE from the contribution column; snapshot DORMANT because the snapshot's challenge weight carries no substrate variance) — see reconciliation note.

sealer_activation_profile_SNAPSHOT._source: sealed_runs.frozen_stats.activation_profile for seal-bae44d1a (the AUTHORITATIVE sealed ADC verdict). schema=adc-perdim-1.0, classifier=adaptive_dimension_controller.classify_dimension, source=raw_governance_snapshot. These mean/std are over the normalized governance snapshot, a DIFFERENT quantity than the jt_*_contribution columns.

delta_m.rationale: active: substrate carries signal and governance weight is engaged

transfer_realized.rationale: active: substrate carries signal and governance weight is engaged

transfer_prospective.rationale: structural dormancy: weight retained but no substrate signal observed

challenge.rationale: structural dormancy: weight retained but no substrate signal observed

uncertainty.rationale: active: substrate carries signal and governance weight is engaged

zpd.rationale: active: substrate carries signal and governance weight is engaged

reconciliation_column_vs_snapshot: The two views disagree on transfer_realized and challenge because the snapshot's per-dimension stats are over raw_governance_snapshot (normalized weights' substrate), where challenge shows nonzero_fraction=0.0 (DORMANT) and transfer_realized shows nonzero_fraction=0.1834 (ACTIVE with high dispersion std/mean=2.29). The jt_*_contribution COLUMNS instead show challenge as a dense, high-mean contributor (nonzero=1.0) and transfer (jt_transfer_contribution) as low-mean/high-ratio. For the thesis, the SEALED authoritative ADC verdict (Tabel 4.8) is the snapshot view: ACTIVE = {delta_m, transfer_realized, uncertainty, zpd}; DORMANT = {transfer_prospective, challenge}. The COLUMN per-dim distribution is the substrate for Tabel 4.10 magnitudes.

tabel_4_9_threshold_sensitivity._method: Re-classify each jt_*_contribution COLUMN under perturbed thresholds, holding the sealed defaults (alpha_floor=0.01, signal_ratio=0.08) as the center. signal_ratio per dim = std_pop/mean from the COLUMN stats above. A verdict is sensitive only if a perturbation flips ACTIVE<->DORMANT.

alpha_floor_sweep._note: Smallest column mean among the 5 active dims is jt_delta_m=0.0828; transfer column mean=0.0154 is the next-smallest. transfer_prospective=0 is dormant at any floor.
alpha_floor_sweep.0.02: 5 ACTIVE / 1 DORMANT (transfer column mean 0.0154 < 0.02 -> would flip transfer to DORMANT)
alpha_floor_sweep.0.0154_to_0.0828_window: transfer column (mean 0.0154) flips DORMANT once alpha_floor exceeds 0.0154; all others stay ACTIVE until floor exceeds 0.0828 (delta_m)
alpha_floor_sweep.verdict: The sealed alpha_floor=0.01 sits comfortably below all 5 active means; only transfer (lowest, 0.0154) is within ~1.5x of the floor, so transfer's column-view ACTIVE verdict is the most floor-sensitive.

signal_ratio_sweep._note: All 5 active dims have signal_ratio >= 0.222 (challenge, the lowest) up to 1.645 (transfer). Sealed signal_ratio=0.08.
signal_ratio_sweep.verdict: challenge is the most ratio-sensitive (lowest dispersion among actives); the sealed ratio=0.08 is far below the 0.222 challenge ratio, so the verdict is robust to a ~2.7x ratio tightening. No active dim is near the sealed 0.08 ratio threshold.

tabel_4_9_threshold_sensitivity.robustness_summary: Under the sealed COLUMN view, all 5 ACTIVE verdicts are robust to modest threshold perturbation: the binding margins are transfer vs alpha_floor (active until floor>0.0154) and challenge vs signal_ratio (active until ratio>0.222). transfer_prospective DORMANT is threshold-invariant (structural zero). For the SNAPSHOT (sealed-authoritative) view, challenge and transfer_prospective are DORMANT by zero substrate variance (nonzero_fraction=0.0, threshold-invariant). Snapshot active-dim signal_ratios (std/mean): transfer_realized=2.289, uncertainty=0.784, delta_m=0.395, zpd=0.0244. transfer_realized/uncertainty/delta_m clear the 0.08 ratio; zpd's snapshot ratio (0.0244) is BELOW 0.08 yet classified ACTIVE because the snapshot classifier gates 'active' on has_signal + engaged weight (nonzero_fraction=1.0, weight_mean=0.1954), not on the std/mean ratio for a low-variance high-mean dim. This is a classifier-rule nuance, not a flippable threshold margin: zpd stays ACTIVE under any ratio threshold in the snapshot view because its activation is presence-gated.
tabel_4_9_threshold_sensitivity.DATA_NOTE: A dedicated threshold-sensitivity grid artifact (research_validation/reports/**threshold**) is UNAVAILABLE (no such file exists). The sweep above is computed analytically from the sealed per-dim stats + sealed thresholds; it is exact (re-classification is a pure function), not a new run.

selector_geometry._source: research_validation/reports/governance_ecology/h12_audit/governance_ecology_report.json (per-policy ADC profile + governance concentration/entropy + pairwise selector divergence). NOTE: this artifact is the holdout_ecology_smoke / h12_audit (smoke-scale, 48 steps/policy, 9 policies), NOT the 96,727-row seal. Its thresholds: activation_contribution_floor=0.01, collapse_dominance_ratio=4.0, signal_variance_floor=1e-06. Reported for selector-geometry context only; magnitudes are smoke-scale and inherit the same 3-learner governance disclosure.
selector_geometry.per_policy_active_dims_smoke: All 9 policies: ACTIVE={delta_m, transfer_realized, challenge, uncertainty, zpd}; DORMANT={transfer_prospective}. (Note: this smoke audit classifies challenge ACTIVE and transfer_realized ACTIVE, consistent with the COLUMN view, because at smoke scale challenge carries variance here.)
selector_geometry.selector_geometry_observation: Across 9 policies the governance concentration band is tight (0.147-0.163, range 0.016) and entropy band is tight (0.837-0.853, range 0.016); hcie has the LOWEST concentration (0.1473) and near-highest entropy (0.8527) -> the HCIE selector spreads governance weight most evenly across dimensions, vs random/zpd_aligned which concentrate most. JT means cluster at ~0.62-0.63 across all policies (range 0.006).
